generatedSentence: Young Elara, whose honor was as clear as the morning dew, spent her days tending a garden of pretty daisies with her brother. Their small home’s directory was simply a map of local kindness, defined by their relationship with neighbors. She often saw a friendly hedgehog during her morning jogging routine, finding support in the universal rhythm of life. A new edition of a local plastic art magazine, tied with a bright ribbon, lay on their table. However, their tranquility began to decline when a greedy landowner emerged, backed by a computer full of cozened data, seeking to exploit a legal loophole. He was an obese man, obviously a master of deceit, known to hire liars to cheat people out of their property, adding a new layer of distress. The situation became deeply unpleasant, an earworm of anxiety that gnawed at Elara. She feared their quiet life would shatter under the opposition of this powerful individual, whose very presence felt like a constant evaluation of their worth. The conflict reached its peak like an earthquake. A mysterious uprising among disgruntled laborers, secretly orchestrated, led to a chaotic scene at the old railroad junction. Elara found herself scrambling for the exit, clutching a small bag of jeweled trinkets. The loud hooter of an approaching train mingled with distant cries of "Save the babies!", as a massive shark drone, made of onyx, patrolled overhead. A broken halyard dangled precariously, resembling a bungee cord over a precipice. The suspicious events escalated into a desperate flight down the highway. Escaping the chaos, Elara reflected on the new lessons derived. She knew this experience would serve as a booster for her future. A local painter offered her a job, serving shawarma to tired travelers. It was a simple offer, a lighthearted joke in a dark time, but she decided not to overlook its potential. She began rethinking her path, hoping to upgrade her skills, perhaps even pursuing pediatric work to help the younger generation. The bathwater of past troubles was finally draining, leaving behind a new sense of purpose, like a homing pigeon finding its way back from a hidden catacomb. She listened to a hopeful rapper on her portable radio, a powerful new underline to her resilience.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic superhero action style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A determined young woman with fiery red hair in a practical braided ponytail, wearing a worn but sturdy green utility jumpsuit with reinforced knees and elbows, and carrying a small, intricate jeweled satchel. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara gently tending to a bed of blooming daisies, a small hedgehog visible near her feet, warm morning light, close-up shot from waist up. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara stands defiantly, looking up at the imposing, shadowed figure of an obese landowner, who points menacingly at a digital screen, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara sprints across a chaotic railroad junction, a distant train's hooter blaring, a giant shark drone flying overhead, wide dynamic shot from behind. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, now in a clean apron, expertly serving a plate of shawarma to a grateful traveler in a bustling, brightly lit market stall, eye-level medium shot. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: A rope used to raise or lower a sail, flag, spar or yard.
A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.
An electrical conductor or interface serving as a common connection for two or more circuits or components.
(medical industry) An ambulance.
A main, direct public road, especially a multi-lane, high speed thoroughfare connecting major population centers.
Any public road for vehicular traffic.
Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)
definitions.example: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.

definitions.definition: The state of being morally upright, honest, noble, virtuous, and magnanimous; excellence of character; the perception of such a state; favourable reputation; dignity
definitions.example: He was a most perfect knight, for he had great honor and chivalry.

definitions.definition: A token of praise or respect; something that represents praiseworthiness or respect, such as a prize or award given by the state to a citizen
definitions.example: Audie Murphy received many honors, such as the Distinguished Service Cross.

definitions.definition: A privilege
definitions.example: I had the honour of dining with the ambassador.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) the privilege of going first
definitions.example: I'll let you have the honours, Bob—go ahead.

definitions.definition: A cause of respect and fame; a glory; an excellency; an ornament.
definitions.example: He is an honour to his nation.

definitions.definition: (feudal law) a seigniory or lordship held of the king, on which other lordships and manors depended
The center point of the upper half of an armorial escutcheon (compare honour point)
In bridge, an ace, king, queen, jack, or ten especially of the trump suit. In some other games, an ace, king, queen or jack.
(in the plural) (courses for) an honours degree: a university qualification of the highest rank
definitions.example: At university I took honours in modern history.

definitions.definition: To think of highly, to respect highly; to show respect for; to recognise the importance or spiritual value of
definitions.example: The freedom fighters will be forever remembered and honored by the people.

definitions.definition: To conform to, abide by, act in accordance with (an agreement, treaty, promise, request, or the like)
definitions.example: I trusted you, but you have not honored your promise.

definitions.definition: To confer (bestow) an honour or privilege upon (someone)
definitions.example: Ten members of the profession were honored at the ceremony.

definitions.definition: To make payment in respect of (a cheque, banker's draft, etc.)
definitions.example: I'm sorry Sir, but the bank did not honour your cheque.

definitions.definition: A person who hoots.
The horn in a motor vehicle.
A siren or steam whistle, especially one in a factory and used to indicate the beginning or the end of a working day or shift.
A nose, especially a large one.
(usually in the plural) A woman's breast.
A penis.
An owl.
A large cannabis cigarette.
A small mammal, of the family Erinaceidae or subfamily Erinaceinae (spiny hedgehog, the latter characterized by their spiny back and often by the habit of rolling up into a ball when attacked.)
Any of several spiny mammals, such as the porcupine, that are similar to the hedgehog.
A type of moveable military barricade made from crossed logs or steel bars, laced with barbed wire, used to damage or impede tanks and vehicles; Czech hedgehog.

A spigot mortar-type of depth charge weapon from World War II that simultaneously fires a number of explosives into the water to create a pattern of underwater explosions intended to attack submerged submarines.
A type of chocolate cake (or slice), somewhat similar to an American brownie.
A form of dredging machine.
Certain flowering plants with parts resembling a member of family Erinaceidae
The edible fungus Hydnum repandum.
A kind of electrical transformer with open magnetic circuit, the ends of the iron wire core being turned outward and presenting a bristling appearance.
A way of serving food at a party, consisting of a half melon or potato etc. with individual cocktail sticks of cheese and pineapple stuck into it.
To make use of a hedgehog barricade as a defensive maneuver.
To array with spiky projections like the quills of a hedgehog.
To curl up into a defensive ball.
To obtain or receive (something) from something else.
To deduce (a conclusion) by reasoning.
To find the derivation of (a word or phrase).
To create (a compound) from another by means of a reaction.
To originate or stem (from).
To turn the course of (water, etc.); to divert and distribute into subordinate channels.
Of, or pertaining to, conditions unique to the descendant species of a clade, and not found in earlier ancestral species.
Possessing features believed to be more advanced or improved than those other organisms.
Product of derivation
definitions.example: The French language is derived from Latin.

definitions.definition: A wild flowering plant Bellis perennis of the Asteraceae family, with a yellow head and white petals
Many other flowering plants of various species.
A boot or other footwear.
A list of names, addresses etc, of specific classes of people or organizations, often in alphabetical order or in some classification.
A structured listing of the names and characteristics of the files on a storage device.
A virtual container in a computer's file system, in which files and other directories may be stored. The files and subdirectories in a directory are usually related.
Containing directions; instructing; directorial.
To determine with precision; to mark out with distinctness; to ascertain or exhibit clearly.
definitions.example: the defining power of an optical instrument

definitions.definition: To settle, decide (an argument etc.)
To express the essential nature of something.
definitions.example: I define myself as a techno-anarchist.

definitions.definition: To state the meaning of a word, phrase, sign, or symbol.
definitions.example: The textbook defined speed as velocity divided by time.

definitions.definition: To describe, explain, or make definite and clear; used to request the listener or other person to elaborate or explain more clearly his or her intended meaning of a word or expression.
definitions.example: Person 1: Is she good at math?

definitions.definition: To demark sharply the outlines or limits of an area or concept.
definitions.example: to define the legal boundaries of a property

definitions.definition: To establish the referent of a term or notation.
Having a definition or value.
Having extreme muscle separation as a result of low body fat.
Downward movement, fall.
A sloping downward, e.g. of a hill or road.
A weakening.
A reduction or diminution of activity.
The act of declining or refusing something.
To move downwards, to fall, to drop.
definitions.example: The dollar has declined rapidly since 2001.

definitions.definition: To become weaker or worse.
definitions.example: My health declined in winter.

definitions.definition: To bend downward; to bring down; to depress; to cause to bend, or fall.
To cause to decrease or diminish.
To turn or bend aside; to deviate; to stray; to withdraw.
definitions.example: a line that declines from straightness

definitions.definition: To refuse, forbear.
definitions.example: On reflection I think I will decline your generous offer.

definitions.definition: (grammar, usually of substantives, adjectives and pronouns) To inflect for case, number and sometimes gender.
(by extension) To run through from first to last; to repeat like a schoolboy declining a noun.
To reject a penalty against the opposing team, usually because the result of accepting it would benefit the non-penalized team less than the preceding play.
definitions.example: The team chose to decline the fifteen-yard penalty because their receiver had caught the ball for a thirty-yard gain.

definitions.definition: A very young human, particularly from birth to a couple of years old or until walking is fully mastered.

Any very young animal, especially a vertebrate; many species have specific names for their babies, such as kittens for the babies of cats, puppies for the babies of dogs, and chickens for the babies of birds. See for more.
Unborn young; a fetus.
definitions.example: When is your baby due?

definitions.definition: A person who is immature, infantile or feeble.
definitions.example: Stand up for yourself – don't be such a baby!

definitions.definition: A person who is new to or inexperienced in something.
definitions.example: I only qualified as an architect this summer, so I'm still a baby.

definitions.definition: The lastborn of a family; the youngest sibling, irrespective of age.
definitions.example: Adam is the baby of the family.

definitions.definition: A term of endearment used to refer to or address one's girlfriend, boyfriend or spouse.
definitions.example: Baby, don't cry.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man or a woman considered to be attractive.
definitions.example: Hey baby, what are you doing later?

definitions.definition: A pet project or responsibility.
definitions.example: You need to talk to John about that – it's his baby.

definitions.definition: An affectionate term for anything.
definitions.example: See my new car here? I can't wait to take this baby for a drive.

definitions.definition: A small image of an infant; a doll.
To coddle; to pamper somebody like an infant.
To tend (something) with care; to be overly attentive to (something), fuss over.
An elastic fabric-bound strap with a hook at each end, used for securing luggage.
An elastic cord tied to the ankles of the jumper in bungee jumping.
A rubber eraser.
The water used in a bath (bathtub).
Son of the same parents as another person.
A male having at least one parent in common with another (see half-brother, stepbrother).
A male fellow member of a religious community, church, trades union etc.
definitions.example: I would like to thank the brother who just spoke.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man.
definitions.example: Listen, brother, I don't know what you want, but I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A black male.
Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause or situation.
Someone who is a peer, whether male or female.
To treat as a brother.
Expressing exasperation.
definitions.example: We're being forced to work overtime? Oh, brother!

definitions.definition: Something that boosts.
The first stage of a multistage rocket that provides the thrust for liftoff and the initial flight
A motor-generator set used for voltage regulation in direct current electrical power circuits.
Someone who is a fan or supporter of something.
Someone who promotes a town or business
A member of a booster club.
A booster dose.
definitions.example: When did you get your last tetanus booster?

definitions.definition: A term that serves to amplify or strengthen an utterance, such as "really".
A power-up item.
A package of cards or figurines designed to add to a player's collection.
A thief.
definitions.example: The security guard captured two boosters before they could exit the retail store.

definitions.definition: Not pleasant.
A characteristic or property that particular things have in common.
Of or pertaining to the universe.
Common to all members of a group or class.
Common to all society; worldwide
definitions.example: She achieved universal fame.

definitions.definition: Unlimited; vast; infinite
Useful for many purposes; all-purpose.
definitions.example: universal wrench

definitions.definition: A line placed underneath a piece of text in order to provide emphasis or to indicate that it should be viewed in italics or (in electronic documents) that it acts as a hyperlink.
The character _.
An announcement of a theatrical performance to follow, placed in an advertisement for the current one.
To draw a line underneath something, especially to add emphasis; to underscore
definitions.example: underline the important words in the text

definitions.definition: To emphasise or stress something
To influence secretly.
Passing under a railway line.
An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology
definitions.example: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Up a slope or grade.
To rise; to get up; to appear from below the horizon.
To have an upward direction or inclination
To rebel or revolt; to take part in an uprising.
A popular revolt that attempts to overthrow a government or its policies; an insurgency or insurrection.
A written work edited and published, as by a certain editor or in a certain manner.
The whole number of copies of a work printed and published at one time.
definitions.example: The first edition was soon sold.

definitions.definition: A particular instance of an event.
definitions.example: The 2014 edition of the Tour de France started in Leeds, Yorkshire.

definitions.definition: A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
A tune that keeps replaying in one's head or that one keeps thinking about, especially if unwanted.
(originally United States) Short for corn earworm (“larva of the moths Helicoverpa zea (syn. Heliothis zea) and Helicoverpa armigera, which are agricultural pests”).
An earwig.
An assessment, such as an annual personnel performance review used as the basis for a salary increase or bonus, or a summary of a particular situation.
A completion of a mathematical operation; a valuation.
Determination of the value of a variable or expression.
An act of going out or going away, or leaving; a departure.
definitions.example: He made his exit at the opportune time.

definitions.definition: A way out.
The act of departing from life; death.
definitions.example: the untimely exit of a respected politician

definitions.definition: To go out or go away from a place or situation; to depart, to leave.
To depart from life; to die.
To end or terminate (a program, subroutine, etc.)
(originally United States) To depart from or leave (a place or situation).
A synthetic, solid, hydrocarbon-based polymer, whether thermoplastic or thermosetting.
(metonym) Credit or debit cards used in place of cash to buy goods and services.
Fakeness, or a person who is fake or arrogant, or believes that they are better than the rest of the population.
An instance of plastic surgery.
A sculptor, moulder.
Any solid but malleable substance.
Capable of being moulded; malleable, flexible, pliant.
Producing tissue.
Creative, formative.
Capable of adapting to varying conditions; characterized by environmental adaptability.
Of or pertaining to the inelastic, non-brittle, deformation of a material.
Made of plastic.
Inferior or not the real thing.
(of a person) Fake.
An artist who paints pictures.
A laborer or workman who paints surfaces using a paintbrush or other means.
Of or pertaining to pediatrics, the branch of medicine dealing with the care and treatment of children.
definitions.example: The hospital has established a pediatric intensive care unit.

definitions.definition: A pretty person; a term of address to a pretty person.
Something that is pretty.
definitions.example: We'll stop at the knife store and look at the sharp pretties.

definitions.definition: To make pretty; to beautify
Pleasant to the sight or other senses; attractive, especially of women or children, but less strikingly than something beautiful.
Of objects or things: nice-looking, appealing.
Fine-looking; only superficially attractive; initially appealing but having little substance; see petty.
Cunning; clever, skilful.
Moderately large; considerable.
Excellent, commendable, pleasing; fitting or proper (of actions, thoughts etc.).
Awkward, unpleasant.
Somewhat, fairly, quite; sometimes also (by meiosis) very.
Prettily, in a pretty manner.
One of several birds of the family Columbidae, which consists of more than 300 species.
The meat from this bird.
A person who is a target or victim of a confidence game.
Concern or responsibility.
definitions.example: it's his/her pigeon

definitions.definition: A pacifist, appeaser, an isolationist, a dove.
To deceive with a confidence game.
A slit in a castle wall; today, any similar window for shooting a ranged weapon or letting in light.
A method of escape, especially an ambiguity or exception in a rule or law that can be exploited in order to avoid its effect.
To prepare a building for defense by preparing slits or holes through which to fire on attackers
To exploit (a law, etc.) by means of loopholes.
A person who owns land.
A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: One who tells lies.
A swabber responsible for cleaning the outside parts of the ship rather than the cabins, a role traditionally assigned to a person caught telling a lie the previous week.
One who uses body strength instead of intellectual power to earn a wage, usually hourly.
Extremely overweight, especially: weighing more than 20% (for men) or 25% (for women) over their ideal weight determined by height and build; or, having a body mass index over 30 kg/m2.
A banded variety of chalcedony, a cryptocrystalline form of quartz.
A jet-black color, named after the gemstone.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Horaga.
Jet-black
In an obvious or clearly apparent manner.
definitions.example: She was in floods of tears and obviously very upset.

definitions.definition: Used as a filler word, or to introduce information even when not obvious.
definitions.example: You weren't to know, but I've spoken to, obviously, Jim about this, and ...

definitions.definition: A vista or point that gives a beautiful view.
To offer a view (of something) from a higher position.
definitions.example: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.

definitions.definition: To fail to notice; to look over and beyond (anything) without seeing it.
definitions.example: These errors were overlooked by the proofreaders.

definitions.definition: To pretend not to have noticed (something, especially a mistake or flaw); to pass over (something) without censure or punishment.
definitions.example: I’m not willing to overlook such bad behaviour.

definitions.definition: To look down upon (something) from a place that is over or above.
definitions.example: to overlook a valley from a hill

definitions.definition: To supervise, oversee; to watch over.
definitions.example: to overlook a gang of laborers

definitions.definition: To observe or watch (someone or something) surreptitiously or secretly.
To inspect (something); to examine; to look over carefully or repeatedly.
To look upon with an evil eye; to bewitch by looking upon; to fascinate.
The action of opposing or of being in conflict.
An opposite or contrasting position.
The apparent relative position of two celestial bodies when one is at an angle of 180 degrees from the other as seen from the Earth.
A political party or movement opposed to the party or government in power.
In United States intellectual property law, a proceeding in which an interested party seeks to prevent the registration of a trademark or patent.
A position in which the player on the move must yield with his king allowing his opponent to advance with his own king.
The difference of quantity or quality between two propositions having the same subject and predicate.
Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls.
definitions.example: a room ten feet square in the clear

definitions.definition: The completion of a stage or challenge, or of the whole game.
A person who is free from the influence of engrams.
To remove obstructions, impediments or other unwanted items from.
definitions.example: If you clear the table, I'll wash up.

definitions.definition: To remove (items or material) so as to leave something unobstructed or open.
definitions.example: Please clear all this stuff off the table.

definitions.definition: To become free from obstruction or obscurement; to become transparent.
definitions.example: After a heavy rain, the sky cleared nicely for the evening.

definitions.definition: To eliminate ambiguity or doubt from (a matter); to clarify or resolve; to clear up.
definitions.example: We need to clear this issue once and for all.

definitions.definition: To remove from suspicion, especially of having committed a crime.
definitions.example: The court cleared the man of murder.

definitions.definition: To pass without interference; to miss.
definitions.example: The door just barely clears the table as it closes.

definitions.definition: (activities such as jumping or throwing) To exceed a stated mark.
definitions.example: She was the first female high jumper to clear two metres.

definitions.definition: To finish or complete (a stage, challenge, or game).
definitions.example: I cleared the first level in 36 seconds.

definitions.definition: Of a check or financial transaction, to go through as payment; to be processed so that the money is transferred.
definitions.example: The check might not clear for a couple of days.

definitions.definition: To earn a profit of; to net.
definitions.example: He's been clearing seven thousand a week.

definitions.definition: To approve or authorise for a particular purpose or action; to give clearance to.
definitions.example: Air traffic control cleared the plane to land.

definitions.definition: To obtain approval or authorisation in respect of.
definitions.example: I've cleared the press release with the marketing department, so go ahead and publish it.

definitions.definition: To obtain a clearance.
definitions.example: The steamer cleared for Liverpool today.

definitions.definition: To obtain permission to use (a sample of copyrighted audio) in another track.
To disengage oneself from incumbrances, distress, or entanglements; to become free.
To hit, kick, head, punch etc. (a ball, puck) away in order to defend one's goal.
definitions.example: A low cross came in, and Smith cleared.

definitions.definition: To reset or unset; to return to an empty state or to zero.
definitions.example: to clear an array;  to clear a single bit (binary digit) in a value

definitions.definition: To style (an element within a document) so that it is not permitted to float at a given position.
Transparent in colour.
definitions.example: as clear as crystal

definitions.definition: Bright, not dark or obscured.
definitions.example: Congress passed the President’s Clear Skies legislation.

definitions.definition: Free of obstacles.
definitions.example: The coast is clear.

definitions.definition: Without clouds.
definitions.example: clear weather; a clear day

definitions.definition: Of the sky, such that less than one eighth of its area is obscured by clouds.
Free of ambiguity or doubt.
definitions.example: Do I make myself clear? Crystal clear.

definitions.definition: Distinct, sharp, well-marked.
Free of guilt, or suspicion.
definitions.example: a clear conscience

definitions.definition: (of a soup) Without a thickening ingredient.
Possessing little or no perceptible stimulus.
definitions.example: clear of texture;  clear of odor

definitions.definition: Free from the influence of engrams; see Clear (Scientology).
Able to perceive clearly; keen; acute; penetrating; discriminating.
definitions.example: a clear intellect; a clear head

definitions.definition: Not clouded with passion; serene; cheerful.
Easily or distinctly heard; audible.
Unmixed; entirely pure.
definitions.example: clear sand

definitions.definition: Without defects or blemishes, such as freckles or knots.
definitions.example: a clear complexion; clear lumber

definitions.definition: Without diminution; in full; net.
definitions.example: a clear profit

definitions.definition: All the way; entirely.
definitions.example: I threw it clear across the river to the other side.

definitions.definition: Not near something or touching it.
definitions.example: Stand clear of the rails, a train is coming.

definitions.definition: Free (or separate) from others
In a clear manner; plainly.
To become cozy; (by extension) to become acquainted, comfortable, or familiar with.
To cheat; to defraud; to deceive, usually by small arts, or in a pitiful way.
A person employed to perform computations; one who computes.
(by restriction) A male computer, where the female computer is called a computress.

A programmable electronic device that performs mathematical calculations and logical operations, especially one that can process, store and retrieve large amounts of data very quickly; now especially, a small one for personal or home use employed for manipulating text or graphics, accessing the Internet, or playing games or media.
To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
definitions.example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.

definitions.definition: To be unfaithful to one's spouse or partner.
definitions.example: After he found out his wife cheated, he left her.

definitions.definition: To manage to avoid something even though it seemed unlikely.
definitions.example: He cheated death when his car collided with a moving train.

definitions.definition: To deceive; to fool; to trick.
definitions.example: He cheated his way into office.

definitions.definition: Having been deceived.
definitions.example: The people feel cheated by the government's decision.

definitions.definition: (often plural) An underground system of tunnels and chambers with recesses for graves, used (in former times) as a cemetery; a tunnel system used for burying the dead, as in Paris or Ancient Rome.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.
definitions.example: Many railroads roughly follow the trace of older land - and/or water roads

definitions.definition: The transportation system comprising such roads and vehicles fitted to travel on the rails, usually with several vehicles connected together in a train.
A single, privately or publicly owned property comprising one or more such roads and usually associated assets
definitions.example: Railroads can only compete fully if their tracks are technically compatible with and linked to each-other

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.
definitions.example: The lawyers made the procedure a railroad to get the signatures they needed.

definitions.definition: To transport via railroad.
To operate a railroad.
definitions.example: The Thatcherite experiment proved the private sector can railroad as inefficiently as a state monopoly

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.
To travel by railroad.
To engage in a hobby pertaining to railroads.
To manipulate and hasten a procedure, as of formal approval of a law or resolution.
definitions.example: The majority railroaded the bill through parliament, without the customary expert studies which would delay it till after the elections.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.
definitions.example: They could only convict him by railroading him on suspect drug-possession charges.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.
definitions.example: He was railroaded into signing a non-disclosure agreement at his exit interview.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.
(upholstery) To run fabric horizontally instead of the usual vertically.
A long, narrow strip of timber bent and bolted longitudinally to the ribs of a vessel, to hold them in position and give rigidity to the framework.
A ribbon.
A long, narrow strip of material used for decoration of clothing or the hair or gift wrapping.
An awareness ribbon.
An inked strip of material against which type is pressed to print letters in a typewriter or printer.
A narrow strip or shred.
definitions.example: a steel or magnesium ribbon

definitions.definition: A painted moulding on the side of a ship.
A watchspring.
A bandsaw.
(in the plural) Reins for a horse.
A bearing similar to the bend, but only one eighth as wide.
(spinning) A sliver.
A subheadline presented above its parent headline.
A toolbar that incorporates tabs and menus.
To decorate with ribbon.
To stripe or streak.
Connection or association; the condition of being related.
The links between the x-values and y-values of ordered pairs of numbers especially coordinates.
Kinship; being related by blood or marriage.
A romantic or sexual involvement.
definitions.example: They have been in a relationship for ten years, but have never married.

definitions.definition: A way in which two or more people behave and are involved with each other
definitions.example: I have a good working relationship with my boss.

definitions.definition: The level or degree of affinity between keys, chords and tones.
To think again about a problem.
The act of thinking again or differently.
definitions.example: rethinkings of the capitalist system

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, raps or knocks.
A performer of rap music, or someone who raps in any form of music.
definitions.example: He rose from the ghetto to become a successful rapper.

definitions.definition: A sword.
A flexible strip of metal, 45-60cm long, with handles at each end, used for Northumbrian rapper sword dancing.

A mechanical, or later electric, signalling device formerly used in the mines of north-eastern England to signal to the engineman that the cages carrying men or coals up and down the shaft were ready to be raised or lowered.
A bold lie; a whopper.
A loud oath.
A Middle Eastern sandwich-like wrap of shaved lamb, goat, chicken, turkey, beef, or a mixture of these.
(sometimes attributive) Something which supports.
definitions.example: Don't move that beam! It's a support for the whole platform.

definitions.definition: Financial or other help.
definitions.example: The government provides support to the arts in several ways.

definitions.definition: Answers to questions and resolution of problems regarding something sold.
definitions.example: Sure they sell the product, but do they provide support?

definitions.definition: In relation to a function, the set of points where the function is not zero, or the closure of that set.
(fuzzy set theory) A set whose elements are at least partially included in a given fuzzy set (i.e., whose grade of membership in that fuzzy set is strictly greater than zero).
definitions.example: If the membership function of a fuzzy set is continuous, then that fuzzy set's support is an open set.

definitions.definition: Evidence.
definitions.example: The new research provides further support for our theory.

definitions.definition: Compatibility and functionality for a given product or feature.
definitions.example: This game has no mouse support.

definitions.definition: An actor playing a subordinate part with a star.
An accompaniment in music.
(structural analysis) Horizontal, vertical or rotational support of structures: movable, hinged, fixed.
To keep from falling.
definitions.example: Don’t move that beam! It supports the whole platform.

definitions.definition: To answer questions and resolve problems regarding something sold.
definitions.example: Sure they sell the product, but do they support it?

definitions.definition: To back a cause, party, etc., mentally or with concrete aid.
definitions.example: I support France in the World Cup.

definitions.definition: To help, particularly financially.
definitions.example: The government supports the arts in several ways.

definitions.definition: To verify; to make good; to substantiate; to establish; to sustain.
definitions.example: The evidence will not support the statements or allegations.

definitions.definition: To serve, as in a customer-oriented mindset; to give support to.
definitions.example: I don't make decisions, but I support those who do.

definitions.definition: To be designed (said of machinery, electronics, or computers, or their parts, accessories, peripherals, or programming) to function compatibly with or provide the capacity for.
definitions.example: Early personal computers did not support voice-recognition hardware or software.

definitions.definition: To be accountable for, or involved with, but not responsible for.
definitions.example: I support the administrative activities of the executive branch of the organization.

definitions.definition: To endure without being overcome; bear; undergo; to tolerate.
To assume and carry successfully, as the part of an actor; to represent or act; to sustain.
definitions.example: to support the character of King Lear

definitions.definition: A position in which a gymnast is holding their body above the rings (or high bar, parallel bars...) usually with straight arms.
A scaleless, predatory fish of the superorder Selachimorpha, with a cartilaginous skeleton and 5 to 7 gill slits on each side of its head.
To fish for sharks.
A fragment of anything shattered.
definitions.example: to break a glass into shatters

definitions.definition: A (pine) needle.
A form of concentrated cannabis.
To violently break something into pieces.
definitions.example: The miners used dynamite to shatter rocks.

definitions.definition: To destroy or disable something.
To smash, or break into tiny pieces.
To dispirit or emotionally defeat
definitions.example: to be shattered in intellect; to have shattered hopes, or a shattered constitution

definitions.definition: To scatter about.
Arousing suspicion.
definitions.example: His suspicious behaviour brought him to the attention of the police.

definitions.definition: Distrustful or tending to suspect.
definitions.example: I have a suspicious attitude to get-rich-quick schemes.

definitions.definition: Expressing suspicion
definitions.example: She gave me a suspicious look.

definitions.definition: An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.
definitions.example: It was a joke!

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.
definitions.example: The president was a joke.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: A task.
definitions.example: A job half done is hardly done at all.

definitions.definition: An economic role for which a person is paid.
definitions.example: He's been out of a job since being made redundant in January.

definitions.definition: (in noun compounds) Plastic surgery.
definitions.example: He had had a nose job.

definitions.definition: A task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode (especially on a mainframe computer).
A sudden thrust or stab; a jab.
A public transaction done for private profit; something performed ostensibly as a part of official duty, but really for private gain; a corrupt official business.
Any affair or event which affects one, whether fortunately or unfortunately.
A thing (often used in a vague way to refer to something whose name one cannot recall).
definitions.example: Pass me that little job with the screw thread on it.

definitions.definition: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To work as a jobber.
To take the loss.
To buy and sell for profit, as securities; to speculate in.
(often with out) To subcontract a project or delivery in small portions to a number of contractors.
definitions.example: We wanted to sell a turnkey plant, but they jobbed out the contract to small firms.

definitions.definition: To seek private gain under pretence of public service; to turn public matters to private advantage.
To strike or stab with a pointed instrument.
To thrust in, as a pointed instrument.
To hire or let in periods of service.
definitions.example: to job a carriage

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to jog.
definitions.example: His jogging of my memory helped me recall what happened that day.

definitions.definition: The practice of running at a relatively slow pace for exercise.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
Set with jewels
The act of joining, or the state of being joined.
A place where two things meet, especially where two roads meet.
The boundary between two physically different materials, especially between conductors, semiconductors, or metals.
The place where a distributary departs from the main stream.
A place where two or more railways or railroads meet.
A point in time between two unrelated consecutive broadcasts.
A kind of symbolic link to a directory.
In the Raku programming language, a construct representing a composite of several values connected by an operator.
(of roads or tracks) To form a junction.
